title: erigeron oreganus
intro: Erigeron oreganus, also known as Oregon fleabane or mountain daisy, is a flowering plant in the daisy family native to the mountainous regions of western North America. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Erigeron oreganus is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the flowers and leaves of Erigeron oreganus are often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically to promote wound healing and alleviate skin irritations.
